preamble: The phonetic transformation from a voiced retroflex lateral approximant [ɭ] to a voiced velar fricative [ɣ] involves a significant shift in both the place and manner of articulation. This change moves the point of articulation from the retroflex region to the velar region, while also changing the manner from a lateral approximant, where air flows over the sides of the tongue, to a central fricative, where turbulent air is produced through a narrow constriction in the center of the vocal tract.
phoneticEffects: This shift represents a backing of the place of articulation from retroflex to velar

examples.note: Proto-Paiwan *ɭ is realized as a velar fricative [ɣ] in some dialects of modern Paiwan.

sources: Cheng, L. (2016). New Vowels and the Lost Uvular Consonant in Tjuvecekadan Paiwan. Tsing Hua Journal of Chinese Studies, 46(1), 137-170.
